items.before: The current seed (post-loophole-fix in a4723f8) describes the convention as if compliance were optional. It says 'STOP' / 'do NOT run apply_patch' but doesn't tell codex that the runtime will actually deny the call if it tries anyway.
items.after: Add one short paragraph after the gate section: 'A PreToolUse hook is installed in this codex config. If your first action on a multi-file task is apply_patch / Edit / Write / Bash without a covering worklist item, the hook will return permissionDecision:deny and your tool call will be blocked with the message: Tool call blocked by PreToolUse hook: <reason>. The hook is enforcement, the convention above is the cooperative path. Follow the convention and your work succeeds; ignore it and you will see deny messages until you propose first.' Light reinforcement since the UserPromptSubmit injection already conveys this at every change-request turn.

items.before: Phase 1 finding (research, no code): codex's developer_instructions field is rendered in the developer-role context part between permissions_instructions and skills_instructions (verified via `codex debug prompt-input` with -c developer_instructions=...). That's a higher-priority slot than AGENTS.md content, which user_instructions.rs renders with role: user under the marker '# AGENTS.md instructions for <dir>'. Phase 1 test in config (iii) (developer_instructions populated + UPS injection removed from ~/.codex/config.toml) showed codex following the gate on the first try: explicit path-(c) preface, read-only investigation, transition to worklist write. Developer_instructions alone is sufficient; UPS injection per turn is not needed.
items.after: Phase 2 applied: (1) src-tauri/src/lib.rs: added ENHANCE_CODEX_INSTR_MARKER_START/END constants, ENHANCE_CODEX_GATE_PROSE single-source-of-truth gate string, install_codex_developer_instructions() function, and strip_marker_block() helper. The install function writes developer_instructions at the top of ~/.codex/config.toml in a # xmlui-desktop-instructions:start/end block (TOML requires top-level scalars before any [section] table), strips any legacy # xmlui-desktop-test-instr block from the v1 experiments, and idempotently rewrites in place on re-run. install_codex_worklist_guard no longer emits a [[hooks.UserPromptSubmit]] entry — the runtime hook is PreToolUse-only now. run_enhance calls install_codex_developer_instructions after install_codex_worklist_guard. (2) app/shell/codex-startup-instructions.md: rewrote AGENTS.md adapter as a short pointer (~10 lines) deferring to developer_instructions + the conventions file rather than duplicating the full gate prose. The seed acknowledges that mutations are blocked at runtime and points at the conventions sidecar for detail. (3) app/__shell/conventions.md: added a new subsection under 'Coordinate via worklist.json' titled 'Refer to items by id, not by ordinal' — agents must name items by their id verbatim in chat, never by ordinal position. Folded in here because the rule surfaced during this work and is a worklist-convention refinement, same surface as the rest of this item. cargo build clean. Manual /hooks re-trust needed once after rebuild + Setup re-run since the PreToolUse handler bytes are unchanged but the file content above may shift.
